machinery_genes.role: tail assembly coordinator (initiates/coordinates sheath and tube polymerization)
machinery_genes.quote: TssA was shown to initiate and coordinate sheath and tube polymerization during T6SS biogenesis
machinery_genes.note: Lennings, West & Schwarz 2019 review of the Burkholderia T6SS-5. The review states the T6SS-5 'consists of the 13 Tss core components that are encoded by the same gene cluster', with each named in Table 1/Figure 1B and given a BTH_II E264 locus tag. TssA is one of those 13 core components.

machinery_genes.quote: Tss proteins assemble into three T6SS subcomplexes: a tubular system located in the cytoplasm consisting of the contractile sheath proteins TssB and TssC surrounding an inner tube formed by the Hcp (TssD) protein sharpened at one end by the TssI (VgrG) and PAAR proteins, an envelope spanning membrane complex (TssM, TssL, and TssJ) and a base plate (TssE, TssF, TssG, and TssK) anchoring tube and sheath to the membrane complex
machinery_genes.note: One of the 13 named Tss core components of the T6SS-5 cluster (Table 1, BTH_II locus tags given for B. thailandensis E264). Sheath subunit (VipA/B-family contractile sheath).

machinery_genes.quote: the contractile sheath proteins TssB and TssC surrounding an inner tube formed by the Hcp (TssD) protein
machinery_genes.note: One of the 13 Tss core components of the T6SS-5 cluster named in the review (Table 1).

machinery_genes.quote: an inner tube formed by the Hcp (TssD) protein sharpened at one end by the TssI (VgrG) and PAAR proteins
machinery_genes.note: Hcp = TssD, the secreted inner tube. Counted as machinery per the T6SS brief (secreted tube), not as an effector. TagAB-5 was shown to affect Hcp-5 secretion in B. pseudomallei (review).

machinery_genes.quote: a base plate (TssE, TssF, TssG, and TssK) anchoring tube and sheath to the membrane complex
machinery_genes.note: One of the 13 Tss core components of the T6SS-5 cluster (Table 1).

machinery_genes.quote: a base plate (TssE, TssF, TssG, and TssK) anchoring tube and sheath to the membrane complex
machinery_genes.note: One of the 13 Tss core components of the T6SS-5 cluster (Table 1).

machinery_genes.quote: a base plate (TssE, TssF, TssG, and TssK) anchoring tube and sheath to the membrane complex
machinery_genes.note: One of the 13 Tss core components of the T6SS-5 cluster (Table 1).

machinery_genes.quote: Following translocation, the contracted sheath proteins are recycled by the ATPase ClpV (TssH)
machinery_genes.note: ClpV = TssH. ClpV-5 (the T6SS-5 ATPase) studied directly in Burkholderia thailandensis by Lennings et al. 2019 (Int J Med Microbiol; PMID 31378704), where ClpV-5-GFP shows polar localization. Counted per brief.

machinery_genes.quote: an inner tube formed by the Hcp (TssD) protein sharpened at one end by the TssI (VgrG) and PAAR proteins
machinery_genes.note: VgrG = TssI, the spike. Counted as machinery (secreted spike) per the T6SS brief. NOTE: VgrG-5 carries a C-terminal domain (CTD) that is the membrane-fusion effector activity (Schwarz et al. 2014, IAI, PMID 24452686); only the delivered CTD activity is effector-like. The VgrG-5 protein itself is the TssI spike apparatus component and is listed here as machinery, not in the effector set.

machinery_genes.quote: an envelope spanning membrane complex (TssM, TssL, and TssJ)
machinery_genes.note: One of the 13 Tss core components of the T6SS-5 cluster (Table 1). Membrane-complex lipoprotein.

machinery_genes.quote: After high dose pulmonary infection with B. thailandensis wildtype all mice succumbed whereas a tssK-5 mutant failed to cause lethal infections and to proliferate in the lung, liver and spleen.
machinery_genes.note: tssK-5 explicitly named and characterized in B. thailandensis: a tssK-5 deletion attenuates virulence. In Schwarz et al. 2010 (PLoS Pathog, PMID 20865170) the same conserved core gene used for the in-frame deletion/complementation of T6SS-5 is given the locus tag BTH_II0857 (per that paper's Materials and Methods and Fig. 1).

machinery_genes.quote: an envelope spanning membrane complex (TssM, TssL, and TssJ)
machinery_genes.note: One of the 13 Tss core components of the T6SS-5 cluster (Table 1).

machinery_genes.quote: an envelope spanning membrane complex (TssM, TssL, and TssJ)
machinery_genes.note: One of the 13 Tss core components of the T6SS-5 cluster (Table 1). Large IcmF-like inner-membrane protein.

machinery_genes.quote: TagD-5 is a PAAR-like protein comprising 130 amino acids that appears to lack effector domains.
machinery_genes.note: The T6SS-5 PAAR protein is TagD-5. The review explicitly identifies it as a PAAR-like protein that lacks effector domains, and the apparatus sentence names PAAR as the spike-sharpening tip ('TssI (VgrG) and PAAR proteins'). Counted as machinery (PAAR) per the T6SS brief; it lacks a toxin/effector domain so it is not an effector. The other three tag genes (tagA/B-5, tagB-5, tagC-5) are of unknown function and are NOT listed as machinery.

notes: T6SS-5 (= 'cluster 1 T6SS' under Schell/Shalom 2007 nomenclature; also called T6SS1) is the virulence-associated, eukaryote-targeting type VI secretion system of the Bptm group (B. pseudomallei, B. mallei, B. thailandensis). In B. thailandensis E264 it is the only one of the five T6SSs required for virulence (Schwarz et al. 2010, PLoS Pathog). The apparatus answer key is taken from the Lennings, West & Schwarz 2019 Frontiers in Microbiology review, which states the T6SS-5 'consists of the 13 Tss core components that are encoded by the same gene cluster' (Table 1 + Fig. 1B) and gives each B. thailandensis E264 BTH_II locus tag: TssA, TssB, TssC, Hcp(TssD), TssE, TssF, TssG, ClpV(TssH), VgrG(TssI), TssJ, TssK, TssL, TssM. PAAR is added as TagD-5, which the review names explicitly as a PAAR-like protein. Status COMPLETE: all 13 core Tss components plus PAAR documented with quotes. Per the T6SS brief, Hcp, VgrG and PAAR are counted as machinery (secreted tube/spike/tip), not effectors. DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED: (a) the two-component regulator VirAG (virA/virG) and the three unknown-function tag genes tagA/B-5, tagB-5, tagC-5 -- regulatory/accessory, not core apparatus; (b) the VgrG-5 C-terminal domain (CTD) and its delivered membrane-fusion activity (Schwarz et al. 2014, IAI) -- the CTD is the effector-like cargo; the VgrG-5 spike protein itself is listed as machinery; (c) this instance's curated effectors (BTH_II0865, BTH_II0866, BTH_II0874-0876). LOCUS-TAG CAUTION: gene NAMES only are recorded here per task rules; I did not mine the RefSeq genome. The review's Table 1 maps some apparatus genes to BTH_II locus tags that fall in the same numeric region as this instance's curated effectors (e.g. it places baseplate genes near BTH_II0865-0867). Resolving each gene name to a definitive locus_tag/coordinate is the separate downstream scripted step; any name-vs-tag conflict should be reconciled there, not here. All quotes are verbatim from the cited papers (Lennings 2019 full text via PMC6335564; Schwarz 2010 full text via PMC2928800). DOIs verified to resolve and PMIDs confirmed via get_article_metadata.
